Effects of AP-PLGA-NPs against cerebral
ischaemia-reperfusion injury in rats
Effects of AP-PLGA-NPs on neurological deﬁcit
score and infarct volume
The neurological deﬁcit scores and infarct volume of
MCAO rats were signiﬁcantly greater than those of the
sham-operated rats 24 h after reperfusion (Figure 7), indi-
cating that the MCAO model was successfully established.
In the IR group, the neurological deﬁcit score was
2.92 ± 0.29. When treated with AP solution and AP-PLGA-
NPs, the neurological deﬁcit scores of MCAO rats signiﬁ-
cantly decreased, which were 2.17 ± 0.39 and 1.42 ± 0.51,
respectively.
In parallel with neurological deﬁcit score, the infarct
volume (%) in the IR group was 40.14 ± 2.61%; treatment
with AP solution and AP-PLGA-NPs signiﬁcantly reduced
the infarct volume by 29.39 ± 3.67% and 20.47 ± 2.26%,
respectively.
